single_responsibility: A parser reads source data and returns typed rows.
A mapper transforms rows into domain payloads.
An importer writes payloads to DB.
open_closed: Add support for a new DAT table by registering a mapper, not by editing a monolithic extractor switch.
Add a new export target by adding an exporter module, not by changing raw import code.
liskov_substitution: PoB-backed and Rust-native extractors must satisfy the same ExtractorSource contract.
Fixture sources and real GGPK sources must produce the same normalized shape.
interface_segregation: A raw row reader should not require asset extraction methods.
A domain mapper should not require direct filesystem access.
A frontend exporter should not require local game install access.
dependency_inversion: High-level import workflows depend on extractor interfaces, not PoB or Rust concrete details.
Mappers receive table lookup services instead of opening files directly.

file_size_guidance.action: Split by responsibility when a file crosses the soft limit during feature work.
